si.t: Reason for Selection (or justification if not applicable) including risk reference
A.5.1 Management direction for information security
A set of policies for information security shall be defined, approved by management, published and communicated to employees and relevant external parties.
A.5.1.2 Review of the policies for information security
The policies for information security shall be reviewed at planned intervals or if significant changes occur to ensure their continuing suitability, adequacy and effectiveness.
A.5.2 Information security roles and responsibilities
Information security roles and responsibilities shall be defined and allocated.
Conflicting duties and areas of responsibility shall be segregated to reduce the risk of misuse.
Management shall require all employees to apply information security in accordance with the established policies and procedures.
Employee Handbook and Code of Conduct
Cyber cell of Mumbai Police, CERT-IN and any other relevant authority.
A.5.6 Contact with special interest groups
Appropriate contacts with special interest groups and other professional security forums shall be maintained.
Some threat declaration websites - Vendor support for key softwares - VAPT Testing scpecialists .
A.5.8 Information security in project management
Information security shall be addressed within project management.
A.5.9 Inventory of information and other associated assets
An inventory of information and other associated assets, including owners, shall be established and maintained.
A.5.10 Acceptable use of information and other associated assets
Rules for the acceptable use and procedures for handling information and other associated assets shall be identified, documented and implemented.
Personnel and other interested parties shall return all organizational assets in their possession upon the termination of their employment, contract or agreement.
Information shall be classified in accordance with its importance to the organization.
An appropriate set of procedures for information labelling shall be developed and implemented.
Rules, procedures and agreements for the transfer of information shall be in place.
Rules and procedures for access control shall be established, implemented and reviewed based on business and information security requirements.
The lifecycle of identities shall be managed.
The allocation and management of authentication information shall be protected.
Access rights shall be granted, modified, reviewed and removed in accordance with the organization's topic-specific policy on access control.
A.5.19 Information security in supplier relationships
Relevant information security requirements shall be established and applied for managing the organization's relationship with suppliers.
A.5.20 Addressing information security within supplier agreements
Agreements with suppliers shall include information security requirements.
A.5.21 Managing information security in the ICT supply chain
Procedures shall be implemented to manage the information security risks associated with the organization's information and communication technology (ICT) supply chain.
A.5.22 Monitoring, review and change management of supplier services
The organization shall regularly monitor, review, audit and manage changes to supplier services.
A.5.23 Information security for use of cloud services
Processes for the acquisition, use, management and termination of cloud services shall be established in accordance with the organization's information security requirements.
A.5.24 Information security incident management planning and preparation
The organization shall plan and prepare for managing information security incidents.
A.5.25 Information security incident response and management
The organization shall manage information security incidents in accordance with documented procedures.
A.5.26 Learning from information security incidents
Knowledge gained from information security incidents shall be used to strengthen the organization's information security.
The organization shall establish and implement procedures to identify, collect, acquire and preserve information that can be used as evidence.
The organization shall maintain information security at an appropriate level during disruption.
Information systems, applications and services shall be made available when required.
A.5.30 ICT readiness for business continuity
ICT readiness shall be planned, implemented, maintained and tested based on the organization’s business continuity objectives and ICT continuity requirements.
A.5.31 Legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements
All relevant legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements shall be explicitly identified, documented and kept up to date.
Procedures shall be implemented to ensure compliance with legislative, regulatory and contractual requirements related to intellectual property rights.
Records shall be protected from loss, destruction, falsification, unauthorized access and unauthorized release.
A.5.34 Privacy and protection of personally identifiable information (PII)
Privacy and protection of PII shall be ensured in accordance with relevant legislation and regulation.
A.5.35 Independent review of information security
The organization’s approach to managing information security shall be reviewed independently at planned intervals or when significant changes occur.
A.5.36 Compliance with policies and standards
Compliance of information processing and procedures with the relevant information security policies and standards shall be regularly reviewed.
Operating procedures shall be documented and made available to all who need them.
Background verifications on all candidates for employment and relevant interested parties shall be carried out.
A.6.2 Terms and conditions of employment
Contractual agreements shall state the employee’s and the organization’s responsibilities for information security.
A.6.3 Information security awareness, education and training
Employees and relevant interested parties shall receive appropriate awareness, education and training and regular updates in organizational policies and procedures.
A formal disciplinary process shall be in place to take action against employees who have committed an information security breach.
Non-disclosure agreements (NDAs) shall be implemented when required.
Information security responsibilities and duties that remain valid after termination of employment or contract shall be communicated to and enforced upon the exiting personnel.
Security measures shall be implemented to protect information when personnel are working remotely.
Information security events shall be reported through appropriate channels as quickly as possible.
Physical security perimeters shall be used to protect areas that contain sensitive or critical information and ICT assets.
Secure areas shall be protected by appropriate entry controls to ensure that only authorized personnel are allowed access.
A.7.3 Securing offices, rooms and facilities
Physical security for offices, rooms and facilities shall be designed and applied.
Premises shall be continuously monitored for unauthorized physical access.
A.7.5 Protecting against physical, environmental and other threats
Protection shall be provided against physical and environmental threats, such as fire, flood, earthquake, theft, explosion, and civil unrest.
Security measures shall be designed and implemented for working in secure areas.
A.7.7 Clear desk and clear screen
A clear desk policy for papers and removable storage media and a clear screen policy for information processing facilities shall be adopted.
Equipment shall be sited in a way that reduces unnecessary risks from environmental threats and unauthorized access.
Security measures shall be applied to protect assets used outside the organization's physical perimeter.
Information and associated media storage shall be managed and controlled to protect information from unauthorized access, misuse or corruption.
Information processing facilities shall be protected from power failures and other disruption caused by failures in supporting utilities.
Power and telecommunications cabling carrying information or supporting information services shall be protected from tapping or damage.
Equipment shall be maintained correctly to ensure its continued availability and integrity.
A.7.14 Secure disposal or re-use of equipment
Equipment containing storage media shall be verified to ensure that all sensitive information and licensed software has been securely removed or overwritten prior to disposal or re-use.
Information on user end-point devices shall be protected from being unintentionally or unauthorizedly disclosed, modified or lost.
The allocation and use of privileged access rights shall be restricted and managed.
Access to information and other associated assets shall be restricted in accordance with the topic-specific policy on access control.
Access to source code shall be restricted.
Access to systems and applications shall be controlled by a secure authentication procedure.
The use of resources shall be monitored, tuned and projections of future capacity requirements made to ensure the required performance and availability.
Protection against malware shall be implemented and maintained.
Information about technical vulnerabilities shall be obtained, and the organization's exposure to them evaluated.
Configurations, including security configurations, of hardware, software, services and networks shall be established, documented, implemented, monitored and reviewed.
Information stored on information systems, devices or in any other storage media shall be deleted when no longer required.
Data masking shall be used in accordance with the organization’s topic-specific policy on access control and other relevant business requirements.
Data leakage prevention measures shall be applied to systems, networks and any other devices that process, store or transmit sensitive information.
Back up copies of information, software and system images shall be maintained and regularly tested.
A.8.14 Redundancy of information processing facilities
Information processing facilities shall be provided with sufficient redundancy to meet the organization’s availability requirements.
Logs recording activities, exceptions, faults and information security events shall be produced, stored, protected and analysed.
Networks, systems and applications shall be monitored for anomalous behaviour and appropriate action taken to evaluate potential information security incidents.
Security Incident and Event Management (SIEM) SOP
The clocks of all relevant information processing systems within the organization shall be synchronized.
A.8.18 Use of privileged utility programs
The use of utility programs that could be capable of overriding system and application controls shall be restricted and controlled.
A.8.19 Installation of software on operational systems
Procedures shall be implemented to manage the installation of software on operational information processing systems.
Networks and network devices shall be secured, managed and controlled to protect information in systems and applications.
Security mechanisms, service levels and requirements of network services shall be identified, implemented and monitored.
Groups of information services, users and information systems shall be segregated on the organization’s networks.
Access to external websites shall be managed to reduce exposure to malicious content and prevent unauthorized disclosure of information.
Rules for the effective use of cryptography shall be implemented to protect the confidentiality, integrity and availability of information.
Security shall be embedded into the development lifecycle of applications and systems.
Security testing shall be performed on applications during the development lifecycle.
The development environment shall be secured and protected to ensure confidentiality, integrity and availability of the organization's information and associated assets.
Principles of secure coding shall be applied to the development of software.
A.8.29 Security testing in development and acceptance
Security testing shall be performed on applications, systems and services during development and prior to acceptance.
The organization shall establish and manage the security requirements for the development of applications, systems and services outsourced to a third party.
A.8.31 Separation of development, test and production environments
Development, testing and production environments shall be separated to reduce the risk of unauthorized access or changes to the operational environment.
Changes to the organization's information processing facilities and systems shall be controlled through the use of formal change management procedures.
Security related changes to information processing facilities and systems shall be tested prior to implementation.
A.8.34 Protection of information system audit tools
Audit tools and information used for auditing shall be protected from misuse.
